family_summaries.note: copy-window kernels look like a credible bounded widening case under explicit slot publication
stable-sort kernels currently look more like trace reshaping than a genuine widening of efficient execution
associative-recall kernels currently show 1 widening case(s) but also 1 refused overflow case(s), so the gain remains bounded and fragile
long carry-propagation kernels currently show 1 widening case(s) with 0 trace-shaping-only case(s)

case_outcomes.note: Bounded slot reads and writes materially shrink replay volume on the copy-window kernel while keeping published state and outputs exact.
The bounded memory tier preserves truth on stable sort, but most of the gain is trace reshaping rather than a convincing widening of efficient execution.
Bounded associative lookup materially widens efficient execution on the in-budget recall kernel while keeping state publication and replay explicit.
Explicit carry-buffer publication materially shrinks replay cost on the long accumulator kernel without hiding the bounded mutable state.
The working-memory tier refuses the overflow recall case because the key-space exceeds the published bounded associative-memory surface.

eval_report.claim_boundary: this eval report classifies one committed working-memory runtime artifact into efficiency-widening, trace-shaping-only, and refusal outcomes. It keeps exact bounded state publication separate from promotion and does not treat one bounded memory tier as proof of arbitrary-memory or general learned closure
eval_report.summary: Working-memory tier eval report now classifies 5 committed cases across 4 workload families, keeping 3 widening cases, 1 trace-shaping-only cases, and 1 refused cases explicit.

follow_on_unknowns: whether bounded working-memory publication stays exact on wider sort and graph-locality families instead of only shrinking trace volume
whether associative recall can widen beyond the current bounded key-space without collapsing into opaque external lookup semantics
whether write-heavy working-memory tiers preserve enough replay and lineage truth to justify anything beyond research-only posture
claim_boundary: this summary keeps the working-memory tier as a research-only Psionic-owned architecture surface. Widening families, trace-shaping-only families, and fragile refusal boundaries stay explicit, and none of them widen served capability or imply arbitrary-memory closure
summary: Working-memory tier summary now marks 3 widening workload families, 1 trace-shaping-only workload families, 1 fragile widening families, and 1 refused bounded cases.
